slides.title: My Five Senses
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 6: My Five Senses. In this unit, your child learns about their five senses — seeing, hearing, smelling, tasting and touching — and the body parts we use for each one. They learn describing words (soft, loud, sweet, cold) and start to compare things (a tree is tall, a building is taller). Most of all, they explore the world around them and learn to be curious and thankful for all the wonderful things they can sense. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name the five senses: see, hear, smell, taste, touch
Point to and name the body parts we use: eyes, ears, nose, tongue, hands
Say which sense goes with which body part (“I see with my eyes”)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name the five senses: see, hear, smell, taste, touch. you will also point to and name the body parts we use: eyes, ears, nose, tongue, hands. you will also say which sense goes with which body part (“i see with my eyes”). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: see, hear, smell, taste, touch, eyes. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Amal at the Market and Talk about My Five Senses. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can see with my eyes.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can see with my eyes. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 - Listen and point. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
